topic: Trump Criticizes DHS Decision to Pause Immigration Traffic Stops
headline: TRUMP ADMINISTRATION PAUSES ASYLUM CLAIMS, SCRUTINIZES MIGRANTS FROM 19 COUNTRIES IN NEW IMMIGRATION BAN
summary: The Trump administration has announced a pause on asylum claims and extra scrutiny of migrants from 19 countries. The US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) has paused all pending asylum applications. The administration has also stopped resettling refugees and has directed the Defense Department to assist with detention and transportation. Deportation flights are being carried out by military cargo planes.

Six big immigration changes under Trump - and their ...

sections.paragraph: The Trump administration has made significant changes to US immigration policy, with multiple executive orders and announcements aimed at overhauling the system. One notable change requires foreigners seeking to adjust their immigration status to secure green cards to do so from outside the country via the state department, a move that has been criticized by aid groups and immigration attorneys. This change marks a shift from a 60-year-old process and is part of a broader effort to crack down on undocumented migrants. The administration has also announced a pause on asylum claims and extra scrutiny of migrants from certain countries, with the White House directing USCIS to consider relevant factors on a case-by-case basis. The impact of these changes is still being implemented, with some efforts already being publicized, such as deportation flights carried out by military cargo planes. The administration's immigration policy has been marked by a series of significant actions, with some sources noting that the full extent of the plan is still unclear.

Trump's sweeping changes to US immigration policy

sections.paragraph: The Trump administration's immigration policies have had a significant impact on geopolitics, with many of the changes being felt immediately. President Trump signed 10 executive orders on immigration during his first week in office, aiming to carry out promises of mass deportations and border security. The policies have generated fear in immigrant communities and have been met with resistance, particularly among so-called Dreamers, who were brought to the US as children. The US has also seen a stand-off with other nations, such as Colombia, over deportations, with the first flight of deportees arriving in Colombia. Additionally, the federal government has disabled its CBP One phone app's appointment system, effectively slamming the door shut on many would-be newcomers. The policies have cast a spectre over many immigrants already living in the US, with some vowing to fight back against the changes.

Trump’s blitz of new policies gives anti-immigration beliefs a troubling platform | Trump administration | The Guardian
How Trump's immigration policies could cost the economy

sections.paragraph: The Trump administration's immigration raids are having a profound social impact, with many individuals and communities feeling targeted and living in fear. Some, like Carlos, believe they are being targeted because of their skin color, a claim the administration denies. The raids have also led to a change in daily life for many, with some, like Carlos, feeling forced to stay indoors and others receiving food deliveries from churches and immigrant rights groups. The Catholic Church has also spoken out against the raids, with US Catholic bishops decrying the actions and a Salvadoran bishop sharing his personal experience of crossing the border illegally. Meanwhile, immigrant rights activists, such as Sister Norma Pimentel, are working to provide support and keep hope alive for struggling families. The issue has sparked widespread concern and calls for independent probes into incidents involving immigration agents.
